Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 15:02 ET
Dealers hold $6B positive gamma as institutions hedge via split P/C flows
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The 0.75 P/C split (index 1.45 puts vs single-name 0.70 calls) signals institutional dispersion hedging into a positive gamma regime.
  • 2. Financials block trades totaling 50,400 XLF calls target the post-earnings rally through May expiration.
  • 3. Vol divergences across six names suggest single-name opportunities ahead of quarterly OpEx in 7 days.
NARRATIVE — positive gamma dispersion setup

Institutional flow reveals a sophisticated dispersion trade taking shape, with heavy index put protection (P/C 1.45) financing single-name call exposure (P/C 0.70). The 0.75 divergence ranks at the 95th percentile historically and typically precedes sector rotation, particularly relevant given the $6.1B positive dealer gamma that will dampen broad market moves while amplifying single-name breakouts. Financial sector positioning dominates the block trade landscape, with 43,000 XLF Apr 17 51C contracts at $0.53 and 7,400 May 55C at $0.11 targeting the post-earnings rally. Goldman Sachs and JPM report next week, and the technical setup supports the thesis — GS shows an 80.5 bullish score with golden cross formation despite IV backwardation pricing elevated near-term risk. Millennium's aggressive 135% Q4 add in MSFT ($2.49B) aligns with today's broad tech call flow, though the death cross technical warns of momentum divergence. The vol surface presents clear single-name opportunities with implied correlation at 0.08 — the lowest quartile reading favoring dispersion trades. Six names show vol-skew divergences (CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW in QQQ, XLK; TECH BULL/SKEW FEAR in IWM), while DASH leads IV richness at +240% versus its sector ETF. Monthly OpEx in 7 days combined with PPI data today creates a volatility catalyst window that should benefit the vol divergence names.

Layer 1: Sentiment +16Layer 2: GEX +$6.1BLayer 3: P/C split 0.75 divergenceLayer 4: IC 0.08 stock-specific
XLFBULLISH
Post-earnings financials rally
Block trades totaling 50,400 calls across Apr and May strikes target the GS/JPM earnings catalyst next week. GS technicals show 80.5 bullish score with golden cross despite IV backwardation pricing near-term risk. The May 55C at $0.11 offers asymmetric upside past quarterly results.
L3: 43K block 51CL5: GS golden crossL9: earnings catalyst
Long XLF May 55C vs short Apr 51C spread
QQQVOL PLAY
Dispersion divergence play
QQQ shows CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW divergence with implied correlation at 0.08 — lowest quartile reading favoring single-name vol over index vol. Net GEX at +$2.8B near gamma flip creates amplification potential for constituent breakouts while dampening index moves.
L4: cheap vol rich skewL4: IC 0.08 stock-specificL2: near gamma flip
Long QQQ constituent vol vs short QQQ vol
DASHVOL PLAY
Extreme IV richness setup
DASH IV trades at +240% premium to sector ETF — highest on the watchlist — while single-name P/C ratio of 0.70 suggests call-heavy positioning. The extreme richness combined with no apparent news catalyst creates a potential mean reversion opportunity ahead of monthly OpEx.
L4: +240% sector premiumL3: 0.70 P/C calls heavyL9: OpEx 7 days
Short DASH vol via put spreads

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
